login.subtitle: Administration — zokastech.fr
login.title: Anmeldung
login.description: API-Schlüssel (<mono>X-API-Key</mono>) oder Bearer-Token (JWT / OIDC), den das Gateway erwartet.
login.devAuth: Gateway mit <mono>development.disable_auth</mono> — nur mit <mono>VITE_ENABLE_DEV_LOGIN_BYPASS=true</mono> in der Entwicklung.
login.errorCredential: API-Schlüssel / JWT eingeben oder Dev-Bypass aktivieren (siehe <mono>.env.example</mono>).
login.errorDevBypassDisabled: Dev-Bypass aus: VITE_ENABLE_DEV_LOGIN_BYPASS=true setzen (nur lokale Entwicklung).

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.metricsProdBaseMissing: Production build ohne VITE_AEGIS_API_URL / VITE_API_BASE: /metrics geht an diesen Host (oft Dashboard-HTML), nicht an das Gateway. Gateway-URL beim Build setzen oder /metrics und /v1 per Reverse-Proxy weiterleiten.
dashboard.chartEmpty: Noch keine Daten — Analysen im Playground ausführen.
dashboard.chartDeltaDesc: New analyzes vs anonymizations between each sample (~15 s)
dashboard.chartDeltaEmpty: Short history: keep the tab open to accumulate points (2nd request onward).
dashboard.alertsNone: No alert triggered by current metrics (readiness, false positives, load, deanonymization).

playground.title: Playground
playground.subtitle: Analysis and anonymization via <mono>/v1/analyze</mono> and <mono>/v1/anonymize</mono>
playground.insertSampleHint: Mehrtypen-Beispiel (Person, Telefon, E-Mail, IBAN) für die gewählte Sprache — dann Analyze.
playground.mockEngineWarning: Gateway läuft mit <mono>MockEngine</mono> (Go ohne Rust-FFI). <mono>/v1/analyze</mono> führt keine echten Erkenner aus — <mono>recognizer_name: "mock"</mono>, kein IBAN/NIR/Telefon. Neu bauen mit <mono>-tags=aegisffi</mono> und <mono>libaegis_ffi</mono> (siehe <mono>aegis-gateway/README.md</mono>, <mono>scripts/build-gateway-ffi.sh</mono>).

configPreview.title: Request config (JSON)
configPreview.intro: Exakte Payloads als analysis_config_json und config_json (Anonymize). Aufklappen für operators_by_entity und Analyseoptionen.
configPreview.analysisHint: Sprache, Pipeline-Stufe, Score-Schwelle — wie die Steuerung oben.
configPreview.anonymizeHint: Enthält denselben Analyseblock plus operators_by_entity["*"] für den gewählten Operator.

operator.label: Anonymization operator
operator.hint: Same operator applies to every entity type detected in this run (`operators_by_entity["*"]` in config_json).
operator.footerNote: Encrypt and FPE use a fixed demo 32-byte key in this UI only — never in production.
operator.rerunAnonymize: Nach Änderung von Operator oder Schwelle erneut Anonymize klicken — das vorherige Ergebnis wird geleert.
operator.redactDesc: Remove or replace with a fixed token (e.g. [REDACTED]). Not reversible.
operator.replaceDesc: Typed placeholders with index, e.g. <EMAIL_1>. Not reversible without a separate mapping.
operator.maskDesc: Partial masking (visible prefix/suffix; * fill). Not reversible.
operator.hashDesc: One-way salted digest (SHA-256). Truncated hex for display. Not reversible.
operator.encryptDesc: AES-256-GCM blob in text; reversible via deanonymize with the same key.
operator.fpeDesc: Digit-preserving encryption; non-digit characters unchanged. Reversible with key + nonce.
operator.pseudonymizeDesc: Stable opaque token per span (demo derivation). Production often uses a ledger / subject binding.

pipeline.hint: Choose analysis depth. Indicative latencies for a short segment (local network).

l1.title: Level 1 — Regex & rules
l1.description: Deterministic pattern and list detection; ideal for strict formats (IBAN, email, cards).

l2.title: Level 2 — Context
l2.description: Lexical window and heuristics around candidates to reduce noise.

l3.title: Level 3 — ML / NER
l3.description: Neural models (NER) for semantic entities (people, organizations, etc.).

confidence.label: Confidence threshold
confidence.hint: Gilt direkt nach Stufe 1: Spans darunter werden vor Kontext (L2) und NER (L3) verworfen. Hohe Werte (z. B. 0,75) behalten oft nur starke Treffer wie E-Mail; 0,50 ist ein ausgewogener Standard.

recognizers.title: Recognizers
recognizers.subtitle: Catalog <mono>GET /v1/recognizers</mono>; preview via <mono>POST /v1/analyze</mono>.
recognizers.catalogDesc: State from the gateway (thresholds below are local to this UI session)
recognizers.empty: No recognizers returned by the API.
recognizers.previewDesc: Optional filter substring on <mono>recognizer</mono> / <mono>entity_type</mono> in the response.

policies.title: Compliance policies
policies.subtitle: List from <mono>GET /v1/policies</mono>, DPIA report from <mono>GET /v1/policy/dpia</mono> (when enabled for the policy).
policies.empty: No policies returned by the API.
policies.yamlDesc: Policy YAML sources remain on the server / GitOps; the API does not expose raw content yet.
policies.yamlBody: Use this DPIA report (when <mono>dpia_auto_report</mono> is enabled) for regulatory documentation, or consult policy files deployed with the gateway.
policies.yamlDemoBanner: Fiktives Beispiel nur zur Veranschaulichung (Demo) — entspricht nicht den bereitgestellten Richtlinien.
policies.dpiaDesc: Generated by the gateway for the selected policy

audit.title: Audit log
audit.subtitle: Data from <mono>GET /v1/audit/export</mono> (JSONL, up to 2000 recent lines).
audit.empty: No audit lines (empty file or export unavailable).

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Local preferences (UI); real configuration is on the gateway / files.
settings.saveDisabledTitle: No configuration endpoint exposed for this UI
settings.keysDesc: Managed on the gateway (encrypted file / vault) — demo entry only
settings.rbacBody: The RBAC matrix is defined in <mono>config/rbac.yaml</mono> (or equivalent) on the server. This table is not live-synced with the gateway.
settings.rbacEmpty: No data loaded from the API — see deployed configuration.
